commits.message:
mit-styling-aj1.3: The Nesting Chain — one recursion motif, four nested forms

Give the Nesting Chain movement a cohesive signature by propagating the
depth node's strongest element — glowing concentric self-similar frames —
backward through the three earlier nodes, so the whole chain reads as one
escalating statement about containment, culminating in the infinite dive.

Each node now visualises 'a thing that holds a smaller copy of itself',
in a form distinct from its siblings and from every other section:

- scaleword: the punch-word lands inside concentric frame-echoes that
  ignite outer->inner as the node scrolls — the nest assembling behind the
  words, foreshadowing the dive. (Was: a lone word in a field of dead space.)
- ring: recast from a cliche loading dial into concentric NESTED rings that
  fill in turn, the centre counting how deep the nesting has reached — the
  containment idea in the round, no longer colliding with the gauge dial.
- shapemorph: the square now literally holds a smaller copy of itself, three
  deep, all morphing in lockstep — the caption's 'holds another' made true.
  Hue kept in the iris/violet family so it never drifts off the movement's palette.
- depth: unchanged — the climax the others now echo.

Treatments live entirely in each painter (build+paint) + its CSS block,
selected by node.kind; the shared paint loop and frame chrome stay generic.
Colour threads through --acc-iris; the scaleword pop stays gated on
REDUCE_MOTION. Verified in Chrome at 1280px and 390px: zero console
errors/warnings across full forward+reverse scrub, clean scrub, no duplication.

mit-styling-aj1.2: the effect — a chronophotographic motion study (#15)

Give the opening steps-graphic scene its own signature: instead of one tile
that hops through four states and forgets each, the specimen now leaves a
numbered, faded ghost at every step it passes — so by the end the whole path
the single graphic travelled stays on the measured grid. The canonical first
scrollytelling, shown as its own trace. Duplicates no other painter.

- Ghost visibility is a pure function of the step index (i'th ghost shown iff
  i < current step) — derived every frame, no accumulated mutable state.
  [LAW:dataflow-not-control-flow]
- Tile number derives from its array index; colour from var(--accent) (iris);
  positions are field-percent offsets — no inlined hexes or stored labels.
  [LAW:one-source-of-truth]
- Honours bmf's scroll-through note ('hold the initial view a beat longer'):
  per-step 'dwell' weights, front-loaded onto step 1, consumed by a painter-
  local phaseAt schedule. The pacing nuance lives in the section's own data +
  painter, not in shared infra. [LAW:decomposition]
- Threads the design vocabularies: --acc-iris via color-mix for ghost tints,
  --t-shift/--t-veil/--ease-* for the eased specimen move and ghost reveal.
  Reduced-motion is covered by the existing global neutraliser.

Verified in Chrome (DevTools MCP) across the full scroll range at desktop
(1280) and mobile (390): zero console errors/warnings, clean scrub, ghosts
accumulate 0000->1000->1100->1110, no edge clipping.
